title: I noticed the series has a tendency to not show
display_title: I noticed the series has a tendency to not show
wikitext:
[]: I noticed, the series has a tendency to not show..             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sun Jun 14, 2009 2:43 am Secondary characters' whole faces. The admiral in ep. 8, Fuyutsuki's superior professor in ep. 21, the JSSDF soldier Misato debrains in EoE, and others. It seems pretty common in ep. 21, actually. It's an interesting perspective; sort of dehumanizes everything else. Oh yeah, one other thing unrelated--Fuyutsuki has a full ashtray in an ep. 21 flashback. He used to be a smoker.. implied similarities between him and Ritsuko?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jun 14, 2009 3:04 am The ep. 7 montage where all of the people are deferring the decision to shutdown JA is another good example of not showing faces. The effect is precisely what you mean; it dehumanizes them and increases one's sense of dealing with a cold, faceless bureaucracy.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jun 14, 2009 3:18 am Welcome to Typical Anime Tropes 101.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sun Jun 14, 2009 3:53 am There's a difference between not showing faces and constructing your shots so faces seem deliberately obscured.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jun 14, 2009 4:04 am Is this typical anime? I can't immediately think of many that use the device profusely.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 14, 2009 7:50 am Timesaving device. Don't have to do unnecessary design work this way. Though, they did draw up the captain and second-in-command from 08 without their hats on and everything, so I guess that argument doesn't always work. Then again, there are also various totally insignificant characters whose faces you do see (Chinese Dude on Plane, Dog Lady, Cat Lady, Candidate Takahashi Campaign Lady...).

[]: Re: I noticed, the series has a tendency to not show..             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 14, 2009 9:24 am FallenTabris wrote: Secondary characters' whole faces.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Sun Jun 14, 2009 10:16 am Well... they had to save money for the last part of the series, right? ;) As long as the characters' mouths aren't shown they don't have to spend time (and money) animating them while talking...

[]:             Quote Post by Nathan » Sun Jun 14, 2009 10:26 am I actually quite liked those shots, from an artistic point of view

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Jun 14, 2009 2:59 pm This isn't uncommon. It's seen in other shows, cartoons/anime, comics/manga, and movies.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jun 14, 2009 3:37 pm Reichu wrote: Timesaving device. Don't have to do unnecessary design work this way. Though, they did draw up the captain and second-in-command from 08 without their hats on and everything, so I guess that argument doesn't always work. Then again, there are also various totally insignificant characters whose faces you do see (Chinese Dude on Plane, Dog Lady, Cat Lady, Candidate Takahashi Campaign Lady...).

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Jun 15, 2009 2:39 am As I said earlier, other series don't make it a point to mask all or most of the faces of some secondaries with onscreen objects. saying "topic pwned" is also pretty pompous and unlikeable.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 15, 2009 2:09 pm "Other series" being...? Are we talking about a thorough survey of hundreds of anime here?

[]:             Quote Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Mon Jun 15, 2009 9:52 pm Using shadow or only showing half of someone's face has been used in film since film began. It's normally done with the "villains". Notice how, besides Keel, you never see any other SEELE committee member's entire face....there's a reason for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 15, 2009 9:56 pm Fast Tony DeNiro wrote: Notice how, besides Keel, you never see any other SEELE committee member's entire face....there's a reason for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Mon Jun 15, 2009 10:11 pm Ok so my example was shot down in flames. It's been awhile since I watched the show and I just remember you never see the green guy's entire face. Maybe you see the others. But that wasn't really the point anyways. In a lot of movies and TV shows, the villains face is concealed somehow. Shadow, a mask...it's a pretty common film device. The same is also done with minor characters, because, well, who cares? They're minor characters there's no reason to show them really.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 15, 2009 10:17 pm Fast Tony DeNiro wrote: I just remember you never see the green guy's entire face.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Jun 15, 2009 10:57 pm Reichu wrote: "Other series" being...? Are we talking about a thorough survey of hundreds of anime here?

[]:             Quote Post by beast » Tue Jun 16, 2009 1:49 am The Angels perpective. Of the fourteen angels that we see fight against the evangelion we only see armisael perspective and then only his metaphysical point of view. It is fascinating especially from the fact that so many movies have made the point of enemt perspective everything. Predator and Terminator are the obvious ones, but Jaws, Enemy at the Gates and many others. We never know how an Angel sees.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 16, 2009 1:54 am beast wrote: The Angels perpective.
